backend/main.mo: Main actor for the Beast Clipping backend, defining all core data types (UserProfile, VideoClip, FeedbackSubmission, etc.) and including authorization, storage, and HTTP outcall mixins with migration support on upgrade.
backend/migration.mo: Defines the migration function that transforms the old actor state to the new schema, primarily converting AdminMessage records to include fromUserId/toUserId fields and adding the new 'paused' system status variant.
feature_evidence.json: A feature evidence report mapping implemented feature IDs to their associated source files, components, and test IDs, covering YouTube posting fixes, feedback button re-addition, in-app messaging/download, and other UI improvements.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: A JSON document containing one-line summaries of each frontend source file, describing the purpose and key behaviors of components, hooks, and configuration files in the React frontend.
frontend/.env: Frontend environment file containing the Vite Google OAuth Client ID used for Google/YouTube authentication.
frontend/.env.example: Template environment file with a detailed step-by-step guide for obtaining and configuring a Google OAuth Client ID, including troubleshooting tips.
frontend/.gitignore: Standard gitignore for a Vite/Node frontend project, excluding node_modules, build output, editor files, and environment variable files.
frontend/index.css: Root CSS file that sets up Tailwind directives and defines OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark color themes including sidebar, chart, and semantic color tokens.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the Beast Clipping frontend app, mounting the React app into a root div and loading the main TypeScript module.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root application file that sets up routing, React Query, authentication, and renders the main app shell with all major pages and components including login, admin panel, clip management, and scheduling.
frontend/src/components/AccountStatusErr: Displays a full-screen error message for users whose accounts are banned or suspended, with a logout button and contextual messaging based on status type.
frontend/src/components/AccountStatusGua: Route guard component that checks authenticated users' account status and renders a ban/suspension error screen if needed, otherwise renders children.
frontend/src/components/ActivityLogTable: Displays a searchable, scrollable table of activity logs showing user ID, action, and timestamp, with loading skeletons and empty states.
Debug panel for admins showing authentication info, admin principals, hashmap size, and initialization status with copy-to-clipboard and refresh functionality.
frontend/src/components/AdminErrorBounda: React class-based error boundary for the admin panel that catches render errors and displays error details with options to retry or return home.
frontend/src/components/AdminList.tsx: Displays a card listing all current admin users with their short IDs and full principal strings, with loading and error states.
Renders a form to add a new admin by Principal ID using the `useAddAdmin` hook, displaying success/error alerts, and includes the `AdminList` component below.
frontend/src/components/AdminMessaging.t: Provides an admin UI form to send a direct message to a user by Principal ID, using a TanStack mutation to call `actor.sendMessage` and showing toast notifications on success or failure.
frontend/src/components/AdminPanel.tsx: Main admin dashboard that checks the user's role and renders collapsible sections for analytics, user management, activity logs, admin management, messaging, feedback, and system controls.
frontend/src/components/AppAnalytics.tsx: Displays admin analytics including stat cards (total clips, trending clips, click interactions) and a bar chart of videos uploaded per hour over the last 24 hours, fetched via custom hooks and TanStack Query.
frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx: Layout wrapper that renders a desktop sidebar (`SideNavigation`), a scrollable main content area, and a mobile bottom navigation bar around the app's child routes.
frontend/src/components/BottomNavigation: Mobile fixed bottom navigation bar that filters nav items by the user's role, displays up to four items, and includes a feedback button that opens the `FeedbackModal`.
frontend/src/components/CaptionEditor.ts: A React component for editing AI-generated captions with a textarea, character counter (500 max), copy-to-clipboard button, formatting tips tooltip, and quick emoji insertion buttons.
frontend/src/components/ChannelConnectio: A React component that displays YouTube channel connection status and provides a button to connect a YouTube channel via OAuth, using the useYouTubeChannel and useInternetIdentity hooks.
frontend/src/components/ClipCard.tsx: A card component that displays a single video clip with its thumbnail, title, timestamps, duration, viral score badge, and action buttons for downloading or posting to YouTube.
frontend/src/components/ClipList.tsx: A list component that fetches and renders all saved VideoClip items as ClipCard components, handling loading, error, and empty states.
A card component that displays either AI-generated clip suggestions or trending clips ranked by viral potential, allowing users to select a suggestion to populate clip creation fields.
frontend/src/components/ClipTimestampCon: A form component for creating video clips by setting start/end timestamps and a title, with support for pre-filling from AI suggestions and saving the clip via the useClipCreation hook.
frontend/src/components/FeedbackModal.ts: A dialog modal component for submitting user feedback, supporting feature requests and bug reports with title/description fields, validation, and submission via the useFeedbackSubmit hook.
frontend/src/components/FeedbackSubmissi: Displays a list of feedback submissions fetched via useFeedbackSubmissions, showing type badges, titles, user IDs, timestamps, and allowing deletion with confirmation via useDeleteFeedback.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: A passthrough layout wrapper component that simply renders its children, kept for compatibility while actual layout is handled by AppShell.
frontend/src/components/PausedScreen.tsx: A full-screen UI shown when the app is paused for maintenance, with a 'Check Again' button that invalidates the systemStatus query to re-check the state.
frontend/src/components/ProfileSetup.tsx: A modal dialog for new users to set up their profile by entering a display name and optionally uploading a profile picture, saving via useSaveCallerUserProfile.
frontend/src/components/SideNavigation.t: A sticky sidebar navigation component that renders role-filtered nav links, displays the user's avatar and greeting, handles logout, and includes a feedback modal trigger.
frontend/src/components/SystemControls.t: An admin UI component for controlling system state (pause/resume, restart, shutdown) via actor mutations, with confirmation dialogs and a live status indicator.
Displays a sidebar of the top 5 trending clips with ranked badges and trending scores, fetched via useAdminStats, with loading skeletons and an empty state.
frontend/src/components/UserIdDisplay.ts: Shows the current user's short User ID and full Principal ID with copy-to-clipboard buttons, derived from the Internet Identity.
frontend/src/components/UserMessages.tsx: Renders a user's inbox of admin messages with a detail view and the ability to reply to messages via a mutation, polling every 15 seconds.
frontend/src/components/UserRoleBadge.ts: Renders a styled badge for a given UserRole (owner, admin, user, friend) with an appropriate icon and color, supporting three size variants.
frontend/src/components/UserStatusManage: Admin panel component for searching users, changing their status, and bulk-deleting clips, with confirmation dialogs and role/status color coding.
frontend/src/components/VerticalClipPrev: Displays a 9:16 vertical preview card for a video clip, showing a YouTube thumbnail, play overlay, viral score badge, duration, and time range.
frontend/src/components/VideoUrlForm.tsx: Form component for inputting a YouTube URL to load a video and optionally trigger AI clip generation, with validation error and loading state handling.
Renders a styled badge displaying a viral potential score (0-100) with color coding and icons for high/medium/low tiers, supporting multiple sizes.
frontend/src/components/YouTubePlayer.ts: Renders a responsive 16:9 YouTube iframe embed inside a Card component given a videoId prop.
frontend/src/hooks/useActivityLogs.ts: Fetches activity logs from the backend actor via React Query, polling every 15 seconds, only when the user is an admin.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useAddAdmin.ts: Provides a mutation hook that calls the backend to add a new admin by user ID and invalidates admin-related queries on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminDebug.ts: Fetches debug information about the current user's admin status via the backend actor, returning placeholder data for fields not yet implemented in the backend.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminList.ts: Fetches the list of admin principals from the backend using React Query, enabled only when the actor is ready.
frontend/src/hooks/useAdminStats.ts: Fetches total clip count and trending clip analytics from the backend in parallel queries, exposing combined loading and error states.
Provides a mutation that calls the backend to auto-generate clip suggestions, falling back to mock AI-generated suggestions if the backend returns none.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipCreation.ts: Provides a mutation hook for saving a new video clip to the backend, calculating a viral score based on duration and title before submission.
frontend/src/hooks/useClips.ts: Fetches the current user's clips from the backend and provides a delete mutation with toast notifications and query invalidation.
frontend/src/hooks/useClipTimestamps.ts: Manages start/end timestamp state (minutes and seconds) for clip creation, providing validation, formatting, and conversion utilities.
frontend/src/hooks/useContentEntries.ts: Provides React Query hooks for fetching, creating, updating, and deleting content entries via the backend actor, with toast feedback on mutations.
frontend/src/hooks/useDeleteFeedback.ts: Provides a mutation hook to delete a feedback submission by ID, invalidating the feedback query and showing toast notifications on success or error.
frontend/src/hooks/useDownloadClip.ts: Provides a mutation that fetches a download URL from the backend and programmatically triggers a browser file download for a video clip.
frontend/src/hooks/useFeedbackSubmission: React Query hook that fetches all feedback submissions from the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useFeedbackSubmit.ts: Mutation hook that submits user feedback to the backend and invalidates the feedbackSubmissions cache on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useGetOwnRole.ts: Query hook that fetches the authenticated user's own role from the backend, returning null on error instead of throwing.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook for managing Internet Computer authentication via AuthClient, exposing identity, login, clear, and login status.
frontend/src/hooks/useIsOwner.ts: Query hook that checks if the current user is an admin by calling isCallerAdmin on the backend, with verbose debug logging.
frontend/src/hooks/useMyMessages.ts: Query hook that fetches admin messages for the current user, identified by a short user ID derived from their principal.
frontend/src/hooks/usePostToYouTube.ts: Mutation hook that posts a clip to YouTube via the backend actor, checking channel connection first and enforcing a 30-second timeout.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Collection of hooks for fetching/saving the caller's user profile and storing Google OAuth credentials, with query cache invalidation on mutations.
frontend/src/hooks/useScheduledUploads.t: Hooks for querying, adding, and deleting scheduled uploads, each invalidating the scheduledUploads cache and showing toast notifications.
frontend/src/hooks/useSendAdminMessage.t: Mutation hook that sends an admin message from the current user to a target user, using short user IDs derived from principals.
frontend/src/hooks/useSetUserRole.ts: Mutation hook that updates a target user's role via the backend and invalidates related role caches on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useSetUserStatus.ts: Mutation hook that updates a target user's status via the backend and invalidates related user caches on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useSystemStatus.ts: Query hook that polls the backend for system status every 10 seconds.
frontend/src/hooks/useTrendingClips.ts: Query hook that fetches trending video clips from the backend actor.
frontend/src/hooks/useUserRoles.ts: Query hook that fetches all user roles and enriches each entry with the user's profile and status from the backend.
frontend/src/hooks/useYouTubeChannel.ts: Custom React hook that manages YouTube channel connection state, providing queries and mutations for connecting, disconnecting, and checking Google OAuth/YouTube channel status via the backend actor.
Utility hook that exposes functions to validate YouTube URLs via regex and extract the 11-character video ID from a URL.
frontend/src/index.css: Global stylesheet that imports fonts, configures Tailwind CSS, defines CSS custom properties for a dark-mode color theme using OKLCH values, and adds reusable component utility classes like glass-card and scrollbar-thin.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider wrappers around the root App component.
frontend/src/pages/ContentManager.tsx: Admin-only page for managing content entries, providing CRUD operations (create, update, delete) via actor mutations with dialog-based forms and role-based access control.
frontend/src/pages/MessagesPage.tsx: Page that displays the authenticated user's messages, showing a login prompt with an Internet Identity button if the user is not authenticated.
frontend/src/pages/MyClipsPage.tsx: Simple page that renders the ClipList component under a header, displaying all of the user's saved video clips.
frontend/src/pages/OAuthCallback.tsx: Handles the Google OAuth redirect callback by waiting for the backend actor to become available, then exchanging the authorization code via the storeGoogleOAuth mutation and navigating to the home page on success or showing an error.
frontend/src/pages/Scheduler.tsx: Page for scheduling YouTube video uploads, allowing users to select a clip and a date/time, view existing scheduled uploads, and delete them, with YouTube channel connection status gating the UI.
frontend/src/pages/TrendingPage.tsx: Displays a ranked list of trending clips sorted by viral score, with special styling for the top 3 entries, loading skeletons, and an empty state using data from the useAdminStats hook.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Provides utility functions for extracting URL parameters from both regular query strings and hash-based routing, with sessionStorage persistence helpers and a function to clean sensitive params from the URL hash.
frontend/src/utils/userIdGenerator.ts: Generates a deterministic 8-character alphanumeric user ID from a principal string using a simple 32-bit hash converted to base36.
frontend/src/utils/youtubeOAuthDebug.ts: Provides a YouTubeOAuthDebugger class with structured logging, OAuth response validation, and common error pattern matching to aid in diagnosing YouTube OAuth flow issues.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration defining a dark-mode design system with custom OKLCH-based color palettes, glass-effect shadows, Space Grotesk/Inter fonts, and fade/glow keyframe animations.
project_state.json: Tracks the current project state including known issues (YouTube infinite spinner, missing feedback button, messaging bugs) and a list of existing feature fix requests with their requirement IDs and versions.
